You
You are a developer migrating from Git, maintaining a stacked set of features. You have a repository at `/home/user/repo` with a linear history of commits: `A` -> `B` -> `C` (where `C` is the commit with description 'C'). You realize you forgot to add a configuration file that commit `B` depends on. You need to insert a new commit between `A` and `B` without breaking the stack. Your task: 1. Insert a new commit before `B` (and after `A`). 2. Add a file `config.txt` with the exact content `base=true` to this new commit. 3. Set the description of this new commit to `Add config`. 4. Ensure the history remains linear (A -> Add config -> B -> C). 5. Finally, create a new empty commit on top of the newly rebased `C` and make it your current working copy. Note: You should use `jj` commands to insert the commit and rebase the descendants automatically. Do not manually rebase `B` and `C`.
